name: BRENSO
description: Dual analog complex oscillator. Two triangle-core VCOs with through-zero + exponential FM, Lock/Flip Sync, a four-bus modulation routing, Triangle Shaper, PWM + Pulse Shaper, Wavefolder with Ping, and AM/RM. Green section also runs as sub-audio LFO.

controls.name: Coarse Frequency
controls.description: Primary tuning knob.
controls.detail: audio rate: 27.5 Hz – 7040 Hz · LFO mode (green): 0.15 Hz – ~40 Hz

controls.name: Fine Frequency
controls.description: Fine offset. Not affected by Frequency Lock.
controls.detail: ±1 semitone · always live

controls.name: Coarse Frequency Lock
controls.description: Button freezes digital sample of current Coarse voltage; subsequent knob twists are ignored until unlocked.
controls.detail: LED shows lock state (green for green VCO, yellow for yellow)

controls.name: Frequency Scale (LFO) Switch
controls.description: Moves green into sub-audio range for complex LFO use.
controls.detail: audio: 27.5 Hz – 7040 Hz · LFO: ~0.15 Hz – ~40 Hz · factor ~176×

controls.name: FM Deviation
controls.description: Overall FM amount for the per-oscillator bus.
controls.detail: sums linear TZFM and exponential FM · range in Hz (not index)

controls.name: Exponential FM Attenuator
controls.description: Scales the exponential FM source.
controls.detail: fully CCW: no expo FM · audio/CV · default source: other oscillator's sine

controls.name: Linear TZFM Attenuator
controls.description: Scales the through-zero linear FM source.
controls.detail: fully CCW: no lin-TZ · default source: other oscillator's sine

controls.name: V/oct Integrator Level
controls.description: CCW → +0, CW → slew-lag applied to yellow's V/oct onto green (glide/integrator).
controls.detail: time-lag drift · CW: longer integration

controls.name: Phase Relationship LED
controls.description: Red LED displays acoustic beats between the two oscillator frequencies.
controls.detail: visual tuner / fine-tuning aid

controls.name: Sync Switch
controls.description: Three-way sync selector for green synced to yellow.
controls.detail: Lock: digital pitch-error correction · off: free · Flip: inverts waveform direction on primary's duty cycle

controls.name: Sync Jumper (back)
controls.description: Selects yellow's sync source (Lock or Flip) when external signal hits yellow Sync In.
controls.detail: set at build · Lock (default) or Flip

controls.name: Square / Shaped Pulse Switch
controls.description: Selects whether Square out and internal source are the pure square or the Pulse-Shaped square.
controls.detail: up: Pulse-Shaped · down: raw square

controls.name: Triangle Shaper
controls.description: Three-way mix of sine, triangle, and a logarithmic wave.
controls.detail: CCW: sine · noon: triangle · CW: log · CV via Triangle Shaper CV · attenuator per CV

controls.name: Pulse Width
controls.description: Comparator threshold — the PWM amount.
controls.detail: center: ~50% symmetry · CW/CCW: asymmetric duty cycle

controls.name: PWM Source Switch
controls.description: Selects what feeds the PWM comparator: Triangle Shaper output (modulated pulse) or pure yellow triangle (classic PWM).
controls.detail: up: Triangle Shaper · down: pure triangle

controls.name: Pulse Shaper
controls.description: Second waveshaper applied to the PWM output. Emphasises high or low harmonics of the pulse.
controls.detail: CCW: bright highs · noon: neutral · CW: low-harmonic emphasis

controls.name: Wavefolder Source
controls.description: Crossfader between Triangle Shaper and Pulse Shaper signals entering the wavefolder.
controls.detail: CCW: triangle-shaped · CW: pulse-shaped · noon: 50/50 · wavefolder improves when fully at one side

controls.name: Wavefolder
controls.description: Adjusts number of folds.
controls.detail: CCW: clean (no folds) · CW: maximum folds · range split into six layout graphics

controls.name: Wavefolder Ping Decay
controls.description: Exponential decay of the trigger envelope used to excite the wavefolder.
controls.detail: CCW: fast decay · CW: slow tail

controls.name: Wavefolder Symmetry
controls.description: Biases folds toward positive or negative half-cycle (unbalanced folding).
controls.detail: noon: symmetric · CW: fold positive more · CCW: fold negative more

controls.name: Modulation Bus Level
controls.description: VCA on the modulation source feeding all four Timbre CV inputs.
controls.detail: CCW: fully closed (VCA shut) · CW: unity · CV via F.4

controls.name: AM/RM Switch
controls.description: Two- or four-quadrant operation of final multiplier.
controls.detail: up: 2-quadrant AM · down: 4-quadrant ring mod

controls.name: AM/RM CV Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales the AM/RM modulator CV input.
controls.detail: bipolar · affects crossfade between Timbre and modulated signal

inputs.name: V/Oct Input
inputs.description: Pitch CV, 1 V/oct tracking over >6 octaves.

inputs.name: V/Oct Integrator CV
inputs.description: Sums into the green integrator for delayed/glide V/oct lag.

inputs.name: FM Deviation CV
inputs.description: CV over the total FM deviation amount.

inputs.name: Exponential FM Input
inputs.description: External exponential FM source. Semi-normalled to other oscillator's sine.

inputs.name: Linear TZ FM Input
inputs.description: External through-zero linear FM source. Semi-normalled to other oscillator's sine.

inputs.name: Sync Input
inputs.description: External signal drives the selected sync behaviour for its oscillator.

inputs.name: Triangle Shaper CV
inputs.voltage: ±10 V · 7 V knob offset · semi-normalled to Mod Bus output

inputs.name: PWM CV
inputs.voltage: ±10 V · 4.5 V knob offset · semi-normalled to Mod Bus output

inputs.name: Wavefolder Ping Input
inputs.description: Trigger fires the wavefolder's nonlinear decay envelope, modulating the fold amount.

inputs.name: AM/RM Input
inputs.description: External modulator for the final amplitude-modulation section.

inputs.name: Modulation Bus Input
inputs.description: Source feeding the internal VCA and four-destination Timbre bus.

inputs.name: Modulation Bus Level CV
inputs.description: CV over the Mod Bus VCA.

outputs.name: Square / Shaped Pulse (yellow)
outputs.description: Switch-selected: raw yellow square, or PWM + Pulse-Shaper result.

outputs.name: Modulation Bus Out
outputs.description: Post-VCA Mod Bus signal. Fed to the four Timbre CV inputs via semi-normal.

outputs.name: Final Output
outputs.description: Yellow's processed signal after Triangle Shaper, Pulse Shaper, Source crossfader, Wavefolder, and AM/RM.

behaviors.name: Triangle-core through-zero linear FM
behaviors.trigger: patch signal into Linear TZ FM In (or leave semi-normalled)
behaviors.description: Both oscillators are triangle-core with true through-zero linear FM — modulator crossing zero reverses carrier phase instead of folding. Preserves pitch when modulated by bipolar signals. Default semi-normal uses the opposite oscillator's sine, enabling complex-OSC-style mutual FM without external patching.

behaviors.name: Mixed linear + exponential FM
behaviors.trigger: raise both LinTZ and Expo attenuators
behaviors.description: FM Deviation knob applies the total FM amount; the per-oscillator LinTZ and Expo attenuators choose the blend. Any combination of linear-TZ and exponential FM is available on each carrier simultaneously — not one-or-the-other. Patch separate signals into Lin and Expo inputs to use up to four FM sources per oscillator.

behaviors.name: Lock sync (pitch correction)
behaviors.description: Digital correction that uses the primary's square wave to subtly shift the secondary's core thresholds. Fixes tracking drift when secondary is within a semitone of a desired integer ratio. Preserves waveform — unlike classic hard sync it does not create harsh spikes.

behaviors.name: Flip sync (reverse sync)
behaviors.description: At each primary duty-cycle edge, the secondary's triangle core reverses direction instead of resetting. Produces complex waveforms with articulation sharper than hard sync but without the full-cycle discontinuity. Not phase-based — no preferred frequency ratio, so primary frequency can sit above or below the secondary.

behaviors.name: Coarse Frequency Lock
behaviors.description: Samples the coarse knob position and ignores subsequent movement. Fine knob + CV inputs still work. Prevents accidental pitch bumps during performance. Press again to recall the knob's live value — without this, moving the knob after unlock jumps to its physical position.

behaviors.name: V/oct Integrator
behaviors.trigger: green Integrator knob off fully CCW
behaviors.description: Patches into the green V/oct path behind an integrator. CCW: no lag (normal tracking). CW: increasing glide — green tracks the same V/oct but with asymmetric smoothing. Patch an extra CV into V/Oct Integrator CV input to offset green by a variable amount for automated detuning or octave jumps.

behaviors.name: Four-bus modulation routing
behaviors.trigger: patch or leave semi-norm on Timbre CV inputs
behaviors.description: Modulation Bus (green-sine default source → VCA) feeds all four Timbre CVs (Triangle Shaper, Pulse Shaper, Wavefolder Source, Wavefolder). Patching any Timbre CV jack breaks the normal for that destination only. Enables single-VCA dynamic timbre control with FM-like spectral changes from the level CV.

behaviors.name: Wavefolder Ping
behaviors.description: Rising edge excites the wavefolder through a nonlinear decay envelope. The fold amount swells then decays at the Ping Decay rate. Creates natural-sounding percussive timbres from a steady oscillator — acoustic instruments' decay-spectrum behaviour mimicked without an external envelope.

behaviors.name: AM / RM final stage
behaviors.trigger: AM/RM switch + modulator at AM/RM In
behaviors.description: Two-quadrant (AM): green sine only modulates positive half, giving classic amplitude modulation. Four-quadrant (RM): all four quadrants, ring modulation of the Timbre signal by green sine. AM/RM knob is a crossfader between the Timbre signal and its amplitude-modulated copy — fully CCW passes Timbre dry.

behaviors.name: Green as complex LFO
behaviors.description: Green oscillator drops into sub-audio. Its sine/triangle/saw/square remain, and its signal is still available as the default modulator for the Timbre bus and AM/RM — turning BRENSO into a one-module modulation matrix (complex LFO + oscillator) with no external LFO needed.

behaviors.name: Self-modulating timbre
behaviors.trigger: patch Yellow Final Output into Mod Bus Input
behaviors.description: Feeds BRENSO's own final output back into the Mod Bus. The Timbre stages are modulated by the post-processed signal itself. Combined with the Level VCA and envelope, this produces self-oscillating wavefolder textures and chaotic feedback timbres.

behaviors.name: Sawtooth only on green
behaviors.description: Yellow provides sine, triangle, square/shaped pulse, and Final. Green adds a sawtooth output (sine, triangle, saw, square). Asymmetric output set between oscillators — remember that only green gives saw; yellow's sawtooth-equivalent is the Pulse Shaper output.

patchIdeas.name: Complex-OSC lead
patchIdeas.patch: Mutual semi-normalled TZFM between yellow and green, shaped through Triangle Shaper into a cranked Wavefolder, tracked by the V/oct sequence.
steps: Patch Sequencer Pitch CV → BRENSO V/Oct Input.
Set yellow Frequency Scale to audio (green Frequency Scale switch up = audio).
Tune green Coarse Frequency roughly one octave + a fifth above yellow by ear (Phase Relationship LED helps).
Leave Linear TZ FM In unpatched on both oscillators — semi-normal routes each oscillator's sine into the other's TZFM.
Set FM Deviation knob to noon, Linear TZFM Attenuator (yellow) to ~2 o'clock, Exponential FM Attenuator fully CCW.
Set Triangle Shaper knob to noon (triangle), Wavefolder Source to fully CCW (triangle-shaped side), Wavefolder knob to ~3 o'clock.
Set AM/RM knob fully CCW so Final passes dry Timbre.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → VCA Input, Envelope → VCA CV, VCA Out → monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — Timbre-processed yellow signal, ±5 V, tracks V/oct.
patchIdeas.hear: A bright, animated lead: pitched notes come through with a complex, evolving harmonic spectrum that shifts as mutual FM and folding interact. Back Linear TZFM Attenuator off toward noon for softer, more singing tones.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Yellow: audio
* Green: audio, +oct +5th
* Yellow Lin TZ Atten: ~2 o'clock
* Wavefolder: ~3 o'clock

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> BRENSO (V/Oct)
- BRENSO (Final Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-pinging kick
patchIdeas.patch: Clock triggers Wavefolder Ping with a long decay so the fold envelope itself shapes a percussive body at kick frequency.
steps: Flip green Frequency Scale switch down to LFO (not strictly needed, but frees green as a modulator).
Set yellow Coarse Frequency to ~50 Hz (lowest range, left-hand graphics).
Set Triangle Shaper knob to noon, Wavefolder Source fully CCW (triangle side).
Set Wavefolder knob fully CCW — Ping will add the folds on each trigger.
Set Wavefolder Ping Decay fully CW for a long fold tail.
Patch Clock/Trigger → BRENSO Wavefolder Ping Input.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → VCA Input, same Clock → Envelope → VCA CV for the amplitude shape.
Patch VCA Out → monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — a pinged, decay-shaped kick, ±5 V, pitched by yellow Coarse.
patchIdeas.hear: A punchy analog kick: each trigger blooms the wavefolder open and decays back to a clean fundamental. Shortening Ping Decay tightens the click; raising yellow Coarse moves from sub-kick to tom territory.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Green: LFO mode
* Yellow Coarse: ~50 Hz
* Wavefolder: CCW
* Ping Decay: CW

- Clock (Out) t> BRENSO (Wavefolder Ping)
- BRENSO (Final Out) -> VCA (In)
- Ping Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Flip-sync noise oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Green Sync set to Flip while noise jitters its exponential FM — yellow's duty cycle flips green's core direction at wildly varying rates.
steps: Set yellow Coarse Frequency to a mid-range pitch (say 220–440 Hz).
Set green Frequency Scale to audio.
Set green Sync Switch to Flip (up or down per panel — it's the 3-way switch's Flip position).
Patch White/Pink Noise source → BRENSO green Exponential FM Input.
Raise green Exponential FM Attenuator to ~2 o'clock.
Leave FM Deviation knob at noon, Linear TZFM Attenuator fully CCW.
Patch BRENSO green Sine Output → Filter Input → monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Green Sine Output — pitch-chaotic flipped waveform, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: Harsh, grainy, inharmonic drone with constant articulation changes. Lower the Exponential FM Attenuator toward CCW for calmer sync textures; raise it for full noise-music chaos.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Green Sync: Flip
* Yellow Coarse: mid

- Noise (White) >> BRENSO.Green (Expo FM In)
- BRENSO.Green (Sine) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: TZFM modulator sequencing
patchIdeas.patch: Two quantized sequences drive yellow V/oct and a separate VCO feeding yellow's Linear TZ FM — true two-operator FM voices.
steps: Patch Sequencer 1 Pitch CV → BRENSO Yellow V/Oct Input.
Patch Sequencer 2 Pitch CV → Quantizer Input → external VCO V/Oct.
Patch that external VCO's sine → BRENSO Yellow Linear TZ FM Input (this breaks the semi-normal).
Set FM Deviation knob to noon.
Turn yellow Linear TZFM Attenuator fully CW, yellow Exponential FM Attenuator fully CCW.
Set Triangle Shaper knob to noon, Wavefolder knob to ~10 o'clock (light fold).
Patch BRENSO Final Output → Filter → Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — two-operator FM voice tracking Sequencer 1, spectrum shaped by Sequencer 2.
patchIdeas.hear: Bell-like, DX-flavoured timbres where carrier pitch follows Seq 1 and sideband structure follows Seq 2. Drop yellow Linear TZFM Attenuator to reduce brightness; raise FM Deviation for metallic clangs.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Yellow Lin TZ Atten: CW
* FM Deviation: noon

- Seq 1 (Pitch) p> BRENSO.Yellow (V/Oct)
- Seq 2 (Pitch) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO Mod (V/Oct)
- VCO Mod (Out) -> BRENSO.Yellow (Lin TZ FM In)

patchIdeas.name: Snappy exponential FM
patchIdeas.patch: Fast envelope into yellow's Exponential FM jack gives a pitched bloom on every gate — classic tonal percussion.
steps: Set yellow Coarse Frequency to ~40 Hz (lowest range).
Patch Gate source → Envelope Generator trigger.
Patch Envelope Out → BRENSO Yellow Exponential FM Input (breaks the semi-normal).
Raise yellow Exponential FM Attenuator to ~2 o'clock.
Set FM Deviation knob to noon.
Set Triangle Shaper knob to CCW (sine side) for a pure bloom.
Set Wavefolder knob CCW, AM/RM knob CCW.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → VCA Input, Envelope → VCA CV, VCA Out → monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — tonal drum with exponential pitch sweep, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: An 808-style tonal percussion with a sharp pitched click. Raise the Exponential FM Attenuator for longer pitch sweeps; shorten the envelope decay for tighter pop.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Yellow Coarse: ~40 Hz
* Yellow Expo Atten: ~2 o'clock

- Gate (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> BRENSO.Yellow (Expo FM In)
- BRENSO (Final Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Self-patched feedback timbre
patchIdeas.patch: Final Output back into Mod Bus Input with a slow LFO sweeping the Mod Bus VCA — BRENSO modulates its own Timbre stages.
steps: Patch BRENSO Final Output → BRENSO Modulation Bus Input (breaks the green-sine semi-normal).
Leave all four Timbre CV jacks unpatched (Triangle Shaper CV, PWM CV, Pulse Shaper CV, Wavefolder Source CV, Wavefolder CV) so they receive the Mod Bus via semi-normal.
Set Modulation Bus Level knob fully CW (VCA wide open).
Patch slow LFO → BRENSO Modulation Bus Level CV Input.
Turn Modulation Bus Level CV Attenuverter CW by ~50%.
Set yellow Coarse Frequency to a sustained audio pitch; set Triangle Shaper noon, Wavefolder knob ~1 o'clock.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → Filter Input → Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — self-modulating feedback spectrum, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: Chaotic, self-oscillating textures that breathe in and out as the LFO opens the feedback VCA. Lower Modulation Bus Level CV Attenuverter for controlled shimmer; raise it for full dissonant collapse.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Mod Bus Level: CW
* Timbre CVs: semi-normalled

- BRENSO (Final Out) -> BRENSO (Mod Bus In)
- LFO (Slow Out) >> BRENSO (Mod Bus Level CV)
- BRENSO (Final Out) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: Noise-modulated wavefolder
patchIdeas.patch: Pink noise into Wavefolder CV adds random micro-folds on top of a clean baseline — analog shimmer.
steps: Patch Sequencer Pitch CV → BRENSO V/Oct Input.
Set yellow Coarse Frequency to a sustained mid pitch.
Set Triangle Shaper knob to noon, Wavefolder Source to ~noon.
Set Wavefolder knob fully CCW (clean baseline).
Patch Pink Noise source → BRENSO Wavefolder CV Input (breaks Mod Bus semi-normal for this destination).
Set AM/RM knob CCW for dry Timbre.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — clean tone with stochastic fold grain, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: A sustained tone with an analog-chorus-like shimmer — tiny random fold events sprinkle highs across the spectrum. Swap pink for white noise for brighter grain; add a slow LFO to Wavefolder Symmetry CV for swimming stereo feel.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Wavefolder: CCW

- Noise (Pink) >> BRENSO (Wavefolder CV)
- BRENSO (Final Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Complex LFO modulation matrix
patchIdeas.patch: Green in LFO mode provides four phase-related modulation outputs plus the Mod Bus — one module, five destinations.
steps: Flip green Frequency Scale switch down to LFO.
Set green Coarse Frequency to a slow cycle (~1–2 Hz).
Leave Modulation Bus Input unpatched (semi-normal = green sine) and set Modulation Bus Level fully CW.
Patch BRENSO Modulation Bus Output → external Filter Cutoff CV.
Patch BRENSO green Triangle Output → external VCA CV.
Patch BRENSO green Square Output → external Clock Divider Input.
Patch BRENSO green Sawtooth Output → external Reverb Time CV.
Tune yellow as your audio voice and patch Final Output → Filter.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Four green outputs (sine via Mod Bus, triangle, square, saw) plus green sine itself — all running as slow LFOs, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: A whole patch breathes in sync: filter sweeps on the sine, amplitude pulsates on the triangle, gate-divided rhythm from the square, and reverb-time ripples on the saw. Slow green Coarse further for drone evolution; raise it for motorik pulsing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Green: LFO mode

- BRENSO (Mod Bus Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- BRENSO.Green (Triangle) >> VCA (CV)
- BRENSO.Green (Square) c> Clock Div (In)
- BRENSO.Green (Saw) >> Reverb (Time CV)

patchIdeas.name: Classic dual-VCO ring mod
patchIdeas.patch: AM/RM switch to 4-quadrant, AM/RM knob full CW — Final carries only the ring-modulated product of yellow's Timbre and green's sine.
steps: Patch Sequencer Pitch CV → BRENSO V/Oct Input.
Set green Frequency Scale to audio.
Tune green Coarse to a musical interval above yellow (a fifth or a minor third works).
Leave AM/RM Input unpatched — semi-normal pulls in green sine as the modulator.
Set AM/RM Switch down (4-quadrant / Ring).
Set AM/RM knob fully CW (only ring-modulated signal reaches Final).
Set Triangle Shaper noon, Wavefolder CCW, PWM noon.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → VCA Input, Envelope → VCA CV, VCA Out → monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — four-quadrant ring-modulated spectrum, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: Classic bell and metallic FM-adjacent tones with strong inharmonic sidebands. Move AM/RM switch up (2-quadrant) for softer AM tremolo instead of ring; change green's interval for completely different harmonic colours.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* AM/RM Switch: Ring (down)
* AM/RM Knob: CW

- BRENSO (Final Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Lock sync for drift-free stacks
patchIdeas.patch: Both oscillators tuned to unison with green Sync → Lock so the digital correction keeps them from drifting apart over time.
steps: Patch Sequencer Pitch CV → BRENSO V/Oct Input.
Set green Frequency Scale to audio.
Tune yellow Coarse and green Coarse to the same note (Phase Relationship LED slows to a crawl when near unison).
Set green Sync Switch to Lock (up position).
Use green Fine Frequency for micro-detune (not affected by Frequency Lock).
Patch BRENSO yellow Sine Output → Mixer Input 1.
Patch BRENSO green Sine Output → Mixer Input 2.
Patch Mixer Out → Filter → Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Yellow Sine + Green Sine mixed — two locked oscillators in stable unison, each ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: A thick, stable unison with controllable beating. Add green Fine detune for chorused width; disable Lock briefly to hear the drift it was hiding.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Green Sync: Lock
* Yellow Coarse = Green Coarse

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> BRENSO (V/Oct)
- BRENSO.Yellow (Sine) -> Mixer (In 1)
- BRENSO.Green (Sine) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: PWM at audio rate
patchIdeas.patch: Green sine, in audio mode, semi-normalled into PWM CV animates yellow's duty cycle at audio rate — screaming FM-PW timbres.
steps: Set green Frequency Scale to audio, green Coarse to a musical interval against yellow.
Patch Sequencer Pitch CV → BRENSO V/Oct Input.
Leave PWM CV jack unpatched — Mod Bus semi-normal feeds green sine into PWM CV.
Set Modulation Bus Level knob CW (VCA open); leave Modulation Bus Input unpatched.
Set Pulse Width knob near noon.
Set PWM Source Switch up (Triangle Shaper feeds PWM) or down (pure triangle) — try both.
Set Pulse Shaper knob fully CW for bright tone; Square/Shaped Pulse Switch up (shaped) so Final carries the PWM path.
Patch BRENSO Final Output → Filter → Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Final Output — audio-rate PWM'd, Pulse-Shaped yellow signal, ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: Screaming, reedy, FM-pulse textures that morph with green's pitch. Drop green to LFO mode for classic slow PWM; back off Modulation Bus Level to tame the harshness.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Green: audio rate
* Pulse Shaper: CW

- BRENSO (Final Out) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: Integrator glide split
patchIdeas.patch: One V/oct feeds yellow directly while an offset CV into the V/Oct Integrator delays green's pitch — built-in glide between the two oscillators.
steps: Patch Sequencer Pitch CV → BRENSO V/Oct Input (shared with green through the Integrator).
Set green V/oct Integrator Level knob to ~2 o'clock (audible glide).
Patch a CV offset (e.g., sequencer aux out) → BRENSO V/Oct Integrator CV Input to shift green by a variable amount.
Set both Frequency Scale switches to audio; tune green Coarse a fifth above yellow.
Leave Sync Switch off (free).
Set Triangle Shaper noon, Wavefolder ~10 o'clock.
Patch BRENSO yellow Square Output → Voice L, BRENSO green Sawtooth Output → Voice R for a stereo split.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Yellow Square (crisp pulse) and Green Sawtooth (lagged, glided saw), each ±5 V.
patchIdeas.hear: A stereo dual-VCO patch where the right channel lags into each note with smooth portamento. CCW the Integrator knob to kill the glide; CW it for long slow pitch smears.
patchIdeas.diagram:
BRENSO:
* Green: audio
* Integrator: ~2 o'clock

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> BRENSO (V/Oct)
- Offset CV (Out) >> BRENSO (V/Oct Integrator CV)
- BRENSO.Yellow (Square) -> Voice L (In)
- BRENSO.Green (Saw) -> Voice R (In)
